name: Brunswick Stew with Corn Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme or 4 sprigs fresh thyme
3 medium new potatoes, peeled and cubed
1 cup dried baby lima beans, soaked overnight
2/3 cup fresh corn kernels or frozen, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak lima beans overnight in cold water, then drain.
2. Combine the peppercorns, cloves, garlic, bay leaves, parsley, thyme, and 2 quarts chicken stock in a small piece of cheesecloth. Secure with a string.
3. Add the cheesecloth bundle to an 8-quart pot with the remaining stock and bring to a simmer over medium-high heat.
4. Add stewing chicken and chopped onion; simmer, covered, until the chicken is tender, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Chill overnight if desired.
5. Skim fat from the surface of the stock. Remove chicken and discard skin and bones; cut meat into bite-size pieces.
6. Cook bacon in a medium skillet over medium heat until crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towel.
7. Add bacon to the stock and add remaining onion. Saute quickly until browned.
8. Add potatoes, tomatoes, lima beans, tomato paste, red pepper (if using), salt, and black pepper; stir to combine.
9. Cover partially and simmer until beans are tender, about 40-60 minutes. (Stew can be prepared several days ahead to this point and refrigerated.)
10. Stir in reserved chicken, okra, and corn. Bring stew to a simmer, then drop dumpling batter by tablespoonfuls.
11. Cover pot securely and simmer until dumplings are puffed and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean, about 15 minutes.
12. Turn into soup tureen or serve spooning dumplings into heated bowls and ladling soup over.
